sources.title: How Does a Hydraulic Ram Pump Work? — Practical Engineering
keyFacts: Invented over 200 years ago; pumps water uphill with no external power except the flowing water itself
Only two moving parts: waste valve and delivery valve
Works by converting velocity energy (kinetic) into pressure via water hammer when the waste valve slams shut
Redistributes energy: converts low head / high flow into high head / low flow
Air chamber smooths pressure spikes and provides even delivery flow
Can pump water well above the source elevation — Grady demonstrated above his shed roof
Types of fluid energy: gravitational potential, pressure head, velocity head (v²/2g)

sources.title: Hydraulic Ram Pump Design Calculator — Borst Engineering
keyFacts: Key inputs: source flow rate, fall, lift, drive line diameter & length, delivery line diameter & length, material (galvanized steel vs PVC)
Outputs: delivery flow rate (GPM/GPD), waste flow rate, efficiency %, head ratio, Joukowski ratio, reliability %, line pressure (PSI)
Head ratio = Lift / Fall (minimum 2 required, 5+ recommended)
Joukowski ratio relates to water hammer peak pressure; avoid even integers for reliability
Installation efficiency typically ~60%
Calvert criteria for min/max drive line length
Friction head loss recommendations: drive line ≤5% of fall, total ≤10%, delivery ≤10% of lift

sources.title: Hydraulic Ram Pumps — NC State Extension
keyFacts: EBAE 161-92 publication; suitable for household/garden/livestock water where power is limited
Requires minimum 3 ft usable fall from source to pump
Cycle repeats 20–100 times per minute depending on flow
Equation: Q = 1440 × (E × S) / (L/F) where Q=gpd, E=efficiency (0.6), S=source gpm, L=lift ft, F=fall ft
Example: 20 gpm source, 4 ft fall, 24 ft lift = 2880 gpd (2 gpm) at 60% efficiency
Components: drive pipe (A), waste valve (B), delivery valve (C), air chamber (D), delivery pipe (E)
Slam of waste valve → pressure surge → opens delivery valve → compresses air chamber → rebound → discharge valve closes → slight vacuum → waste valve opens → cycle repeats

sources.title: Hydraulic Ram — Wikipedia
keyFacts: Invented 1793 by Joseph Michel Montgolfier (of hot air balloon fame) in France
Also called hydram; cyclic water pump powered by hydropower
Takes in water at one hydraulic head and flow rate, outputs at higher head and lower flow rate
No external motor or electricity required

designNotes: Animated SVG cross-section showing the pump in action: water flowing from source, waste valve opening/closing, pressure spike visualisation, delivery valve opening, air chamber compression, water rising up delivery pipe
Interactive sliders: fall, lift, source flow rate, drive pipe diameter, air chamber size
Real-time calculator using Q = 1440 × E × S / (L/F)
Visual feedback: head ratio indicator, efficiency gauge, reliability meter, flow arrows
Dark engineering theme with water blue and copper/brass accents for the pump
